description: Hand-labeled ad/not-ad examples extracted from HTML digest output. Set is_ad to true/false. ad_type: referral_program | lead_gen_offer | newsletter_cta | subscription_pitch | sponsor_content | product_feature_disguised | not_ad

examples.title: Still interested in Tech news?
examples.summary: The newsletter sends 5-minute tech headlines to 503,294 readers every Tuesday and Friday. Due to high email costs, the sender is monitoring subscriber activity to prevent spamming inboxes. Readers can vote on a poll to confirm they're active subscribers or seek help from support@therundown.ai.
examples.notes: Re-engagement poll disguised as content. High email costs framing.

examples.title: A faster way to shop
examples.summary: ChatGPT helps users browse and compare products by describing what they want, allowing them to find the right product faster. It enables features such as finding products within budget and style, comparing products side-by-side, researching reviews and deals, and uploading photos to find similar products. ChatGPT starts shopping search in one place, streamlining the browsing experience.
examples.notes: Marketing email from ChatGPT to drive shopping feature adoption. Written from product POV.

examples.title: Copy, paste, automate 🤖
examples.summary: HubSpot Media offers 150+ AI prompts to help solo business owners save time and automate tasks, including emails, social media, SEO content, cold call scripts, customer chatbots, data analysis, and personal growth. A free "stack" of the AI library is available for download, with bonus features such as mega prompts and additional resources.
examples.notes: Free download CTA. HubSpot lead magnet embedded in The Hustle newsletter.

examples.title: Unconventional Business Stories Every Morning with The Hustle
examples.summary: Subscribe to The Hustle to kickstart your day with bold, bizarre, and brilliant unconventional business stories from oddball startups, daring moonshots, and emerging trends. The newsletter offers 100% fresh content daily (Mon-Fri) and a Sunday deep dive, without ads or recycled headlines. It's free, with access to business-building resources.
examples.notes: Newsletter subscription pitch. Ref link = referral tracking.

examples.title: Free Guide to 150+ AI Prompts for Solopreneurs and Small Businesses
examples.summary: A free guide provides 150+ plug-and-play AI prompts for solo entrepreneurs and small businesses, covering topics such as marketing, sales, customer service, automation, and personal growth. The guide offers scripts for outreach, closing deals, chatbot interactions, task management, and more. With these AI-powered tools, users can work smarter, not harder, and scale their business without hiring a team.
examples.notes: HubSpot lead magnet. UTM params confirm paid placement.

examples.title: How Real Intent's Intent Data Can Help Real Estate Agents Generate Leads
examples.summary: Real Intent's intent data can help real estate agents generate leads by providing information on the motivations of potential buyers and sellers, allowing agents to target their marketing efforts more effectively. The data is used to identify motivated buyers and sellers, enabling agents to provide personalized and relevant services.
examples.notes: Webinar lead-gen for a paid real estate data subscription service.

examples.title: Refer Friends and Get Rewards with The Hustle Program
examples.summary: Refer friends to The Hustle by sharing a unique link and earning rewards for each referral. To receive credit, the referred person must subscribe using the link, confirm their subscription through an email confirmation, and click on a link in that email. Prizes are available for purchase outside of the program.
examples.notes: Classic referral CTA with prize incentives and tracking link.

examples.title: The Hustle brings you Unconventional Business Stories Every Morning Free
examples.summary: A newsletter called The Hustle brings unconventional business stories every morning to 1.5 million subscribers, offering a fresh take on the business world without ads or recycled headlines. It aims to provide new ideas and insights with its daily curated roundup of unusual news and trends.
examples.notes: Subscribe pitch with referral link.

examples.title: How Real Intent Uses Data to Provide Leads for Real Estate Agents
examples.summary: Real Intent provides leads for real estate agents based on data analysis of internet behavior, allowing agents to identify motivated buyers and sellers. The company uses intent data to help agents generate leads, with strategies including analyzing website traffic, social media engagement, and online search behavior.
examples.notes: Webinar funnel for paid real estate leads product.

examples.title: Top-Tier Tech Marketers Advertise in TLDR Newsletter
examples.summary: Top-tier tech marketers advertise in TLDR's newsletter, reaching over 7 million tech professionals. The newsletters have seen significant results, with $382k in pipeline and a 20.1x ROI, as well as high engagement metrics including 100+ webinar signups and 14k+ site visits per month.
examples.notes: Direct pitch to buy newsletter ad slots. ROI stats = conversion framing.

examples.title: Elite Real Estate Agent Program for Top Producers Only
examples.summary: Only one agent per city is selected for the Elite Real Estate Agent Program, which guarantees a monthly seller pipeline and cash-offer-ready buyer opportunities backed by an institutional buyer. To be approved, agents must be verified producers with at least 15 closings in the last 12 months.
examples.notes: Exclusivity and scarcity marketing for paid subscription product.

examples.title: Exclusive Real Estate Agent Program for Proven Performers Only
examples.summary: One agent is selected to manage the seller pipeline in each city, ensuring exclusive access to cash-offer-ready opportunities backed by an institutional buyer. Verified producers with 15+ closings in the last 12 months and active licenses are considered for approval.
examples.notes: Same product as elite-real-estate-program — duplicate ad from same source.

examples.title: Exclusive Agent Program for Major U.S. REIT Buyers
examples.summary: Agents are selected through a verification process, which includes checking for at least 15 closings in the last 12 months and having an active license with no disciplinary history. To be approved, agents must also have a brokerage affiliation and two deal references on request.
examples.notes: Third variation of the Real Intent real estate ad. Different framing, same product.

examples.title: Elite Real Estate Agents Get Exclusive Cash-Offer Ready Seller Opportunities
examples.summary: Elite real estate agents can apply to receive exclusive cash-offer-ready seller opportunities, available one agent per city, backed by an institutional buyer. To be eligible, producers must have at least 15 closings in the last 12 months and no disciplinary history.
examples.notes: Fourth variation of same real estate subscription product.

examples.title: How Real Intent's Intent Data Can Help Real Estate Agents Find Motivated Buyers and Sellers
examples.summary: Real Intent's intent data provides valuable insights for real estate agents, helping them find motivated buyers and sellers. The company collects and analyzes online search behavior and browsing patterns to identify potential leads.
examples.notes: Fifth variation of the same Real Intent webinar/product funnel.

examples.title: The Sequence Opinion #864: Every AI Agent Needs a Computer
examples.summary: Access to a computer is crucial for the next phase of AI agents, making them more than just brilliant brains in a jar. An agent needs a safe, isolated space with tools like a terminal, browser, and package manager to write code, run commands, and interact with its environment. This "Brain in the Jar Problem" highlights the need for a physical platform for intelligence to operate effectively.
examples.notes: Opinion/analysis piece on AI agent architecture.

examples.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash ⚡️, Karpathy joins Anthropic 🧑‍💻, OpenAI Guaranteed Capacity ⚡
examples.summary: Google has introduced Gemini 3.5 Flash, a new model focused on agentic workflows, coding, and long-horizon task execution. OpenAI has announced a new Guaranteed Capacity offering for customers to secure compute resources. Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic.
examples.notes: Multi-item news digest. Neutral third-party reporting.

examples.title: Asset Studio is entering a new era of AI-powered creativity.
examples.summary: Google Ads is introducing new multimodal capabilities in its Asset Studio, enhancing the platform's AI-powered creativity features.
examples.notes: Product announcement from Google's official blog. Informational, no CTA to buy.

examples.title: Flurry of Deals Reaffirms Sizzling AI Market
examples.summary: A flurry of deals has reaffirmed the sizzling AI market, involving some of the most prominent brands in generative AI.
examples.notes: Third-party journalism. Market coverage.

examples.title: OpenAI GPT-5.6 Solves 80-Year-Old Erdős Planar Unit Distance Problem
examples.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model solved the 80-year-old Erdős planar unit distance problem for under $1000, disproving a long-standing belief in discrete geometry, with validation from mathematicians and researchers.
examples.notes: Research news. Specific technical achievement reported by third party.

examples.title: Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic: What Happens Next
examples.summary: Andrej Karpathy, a prominent figure in AI research and founder of Oraetx, has joined Anthropic, a leading artificial intelligence company. In his new role, Karpathy will focus on developing AI capabilities that can be used to benefit humanity.
examples.notes: Industry news. Third-party analysis of a hiring announcement.

examples.title: Everything new in our Google AI subscriptions, fresh from I/O 2026
examples.summary: Google has announced new features for its AI subscriptions at I/O 2026. The updated plans come with additional perks, all while maintaining the existing pricing.
examples.notes: Product announcement blog. Borderline — from Google's own blog, but a factual announcement with no direct purchase CTA.

examples.title: Every AI Agent Needs Access to a Computer Architecturally
examples.summary: Agentic sandboxes refer to virtual environments designed to test and refine autonomous decision-making in AI systems without harming humans or the environment. These sandboxes allow developers to simulate real-world scenarios, making it possible to train and validate AI agents.
examples.notes: RSS version of same opinion article. Technical analysis.

examples.title: 📦  Return to sender
examples.summary: A handyman in Georgia installed a two-gallon, one-piston power washer engine into a pink Power Wheels Barbie Dream Camper. Gas prices continue to soar, but the toy car does the trick for quick errands.
examples.notes: Off-topic but not an ad. Quirky content from The Hustle newsletter. No CTA, no product pitch.
